response.semanticDescription: The Button component is a highly versatile and customizable UI element designed for initiating various user interactions. It supports different visual `variants` (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and `sizes` (default, sm, lg, icon) to fit diverse contexts within an application. Key features include a `loading` prop to indicate ongoing asynchronous operations, automatically disabling the button, and an `asChild` prop that allows the button's styles and behavior to be applied to a different underlying HTML element (e.g., an `<a>` tag for navigation or a custom component), leveraging Radix UI's Slot primitive for maximum flexibility. This component is essential for user input, form submissions, navigation, and triggering actions like opening modals or confirming decisions, ensuring a consistent and accessible interactive experience.

guidance.accessibility: Handles standard keyboard interactions (Space, Enter) and focus management. When `loading` is true, the button is disabled and `aria-disabled` is set. If `asChild` is used, ensure the child element (e.g., `<a>`) has appropriate ARIA attributes if it performs a role other than its native semantic meaning. Ensure sufficient color contrast for all variants and focus states.
guidance.whenNotToUse: For purely decorative elements or static text that doesn't trigger an action. Do not use for headings or non-interactive content. Use a `div` or `span` for non-interactive display.
guidance.whenToUse: To trigger immediate actions, submit forms, navigate between pages, or perform operations that require user interaction. Use for primary and secondary actions across your application.

minimal.title: Basic Button
minimal.description: A standard button with default styling.

advanced.description: An icon button wrapped in a framework-specific Link component, maintaining button styles and accessibility for navigation.
advanced.title: Icon Button as Child

common.description: A large button with a destructive visual style, suitable for irreversible actions.
common.title: Destructive Large Button

common.description: A button displaying a loading indicator and disabled state during an asynchronous operation.
common.title: Loading Button

common.description: Renders the button's styles on an anchor tag, useful for navigation while maintaining button appearance.
common.title: Button as a Link

response.description: A versatile button component for triggering user actions, form submissions, and navigation, with built-in loading states and flexible rendering.
